name: me/mmu
source: CODATA 2022 (via PDG 2024 muon listings, pdgLive S004): muon/electron mass ratio = 206.7682827(46)
notes: Computed as the reciprocal of the CODATA 2022 muon-electron mass ratio 206.7682827(46), which is measured directly (via muonium spectroscopy / g-2 experiments) and is far more precise than combining separately-quoted MeV masses. rel_unc equals that of the ratio itself, 2.2e-8.

name: me/mtau
source: PDG 2024 (S. Navas et al., Phys. Rev. D 110, 030001 (2024)); electron mass CODATA 2022 = 0.51099895069(16) MeV; tau mass pdgLive S035M 'OUR AVERAGE' = 1776.93(9) MeV
notes: Electron mass uncertainty (rel ~3e-10) is negligible; ratio uncertainty is entirely dominated by the tau mass measurement (rel 5.07e-5), propagated in quadrature.

name: mmu/mtau
source: PDG 2024 (S. Navas et al., Phys. Rev. D 110, 030001 (2024)); muon mass pdgLive = 105.6583755(23) MeV; tau mass pdgLive S035M 'OUR AVERAGE' = 1776.93(9) MeV
notes: Muon mass uncertainty (rel ~2.2e-8) is negligible; ratio uncertainty is entirely dominated by the tau mass measurement (rel 5.07e-5), propagated in quadrature.

name: alpha(0)^-1
source: CODATA 2022 recommended value (NIST, physics.nist.gov/cgi-bin/cuu/Value?alphinv), Mohr et al., Rev. Mod. Phys. 2024
notes: Zero-momentum (low-energy) fine-structure constant inverse, measured via electron g-2 and atom-recoil (Rb/Cs) experiments. CODATA 2022 supersedes the CODATA 2018 value 137.035999084(21) still quoted in some PDG 2024 constants-review text; the 2022 value is used here as the current CODATA standard.

name: alpha_s(M_Z)
source: PDG 2024 world average (S. Navas et al., Phys. Rev. D 110, 030001 (2024), QCD review)
notes: World average of the strong coupling constant at the Z pole from lattice, tau decays, DIS, e+e- event shapes, and electroweak fits.

name: sin^2(theta_W)_MSbar(M_Z)
source: PDG 2024 Electroweak Model review (rpp2024-rev-standard-model.pdf), Table 10.2: š²_Z ≡ sin²θ̂_W(M_Z) = 0.23129(4)
notes: This is the MS-bar-scheme mixing angle evaluated at the Z pole, distinct from the on-shell value s²_W = 0.22348(10) and the effective leptonic angle s̄²_ℓ = 0.23161(4) quoted in the same table; the task specifically requests the MS-bar definition.

name: m_W/m_Z
source: PDG 2024 world averages: m_W = 80.3692(133) GeV (LEP2+D0+ATLAS+LHCb combination excluding the 2022 CDF Run-II outlier, May 2024), m_Z = 91.1879(20) GeV (pdgLive S044M, LEP+CDF combination)
notes: A single WebFetch pass returned an unverified 'm_W = 80.3625(77) GeV, 2026 combination' figure attributed to a supposed May-2026 update; a targeted follow-up search could not corroborate this number in any indexed PDG/arXiv page, so it was treated as an unverified/likely-hallucinated artifact of the fetch-summarization step and discarded in favor of the well cross-referenced 80.3692(133) GeV value (confirmed independently via pdgLive, Wikipedia, and the PDG W-mass review). ATLAS 2024 (80.3665±0.0159) and CMS 2025 (80.3602±0.0099) measurements exist but are not yet folded into the official PDG world average as of the sources checked.

name: Koide_Q
source: Computed from PDG/CODATA lepton masses: m_e = 0.51099895069(16) MeV (CODATA 2022), m_mu = 105.6583755(23) MeV (PDG 2024), m_tau = 1776.93(9) MeV (PDG 2024, pdgLive S035M 'OUR AVERAGE')
notes: Q = (me+mmu+mtau)/(sqrt(me)+sqrt(mmu)+sqrt(mtau))^2, all masses in MeV. Uncertainty propagated numerically (central differences on each mass); dominated almost entirely by the tau-mass uncertainty (mu and e contribute negligibly). Central value differs from the famous Koide coincidence value 2/3 = 0.666666... by about 0.43 sigma, i.e. fully consistent with it given current tau-mass precision.

name: m_p/m_e
notes: Directly measured proton-to-electron mass ratio, one of the most precisely known dimensionless constants.

name: |Vus|
source: PDG 2024 CKM Quark-Mixing Matrix review (rpp2024-rev-ckm-matrix.pdf), Eq. 12.8
notes: From K_l3 semileptonic kaon decays combined with lattice QCD form factors.

name: |Vcb|
source: PDG 2024 CKM Quark-Mixing Matrix review (rpp2024-rev-ckm-matrix.pdf), Eq. 12.11 (combined average)
notes: PDG quotes an inclusive determination (42.2±0.5)x10^-3 and an exclusive determination (39.8±0.6)x10^-3 that are in mild tension; the official combined/inflated-uncertainty average is (41.1±1.2)x10^-3, used here.

name: |Vub|
source: PDG 2024 CKM Quark-Mixing Matrix review (rpp2024-rev-ckm-matrix.pdf), Eq. 12.12 (combined average)
notes: PDG quotes an inclusive determination (4.13 +0.16/-0.17? combined ~0.12-0.18)x10^-3 and an exclusive determination (3.67±0.09±0.12)x10^-3, combined to (3.82±0.20)x10^-3, used here. A B->tau-nu-based determination (4.11±0.39)x10^-3 is consistent.

name: sin^2(theta_12)
source: NuFIT 6.0 (2024) global fit, Normal Ordering, table including Super-Kamiokande atmospheric data (nu-fit.org/sites/default/files/v60.tbl-parameters.pdf)
notes: Best fit 0.308 (+0.012/-0.011); solar mixing angle, dominated by solar-neutrino and KamLAND data, essentially unaffected by inclusion of SK atmospheric data (0.307 without SK).

name: sin^2(theta_23)
source: NuFIT 6.0 (2024) global fit, Normal Ordering, table including Super-Kamiokande atmospheric data (nu-fit.org/sites/default/files/v60.tbl-parameters.pdf)
notes: Best fit 0.470 (+0.017/-0.013), lower octant, when SK atmospheric data is included in the global fit. Without SK atmospheric data the fit instead prefers the upper octant, 0.561 (+0.012/-0.015) -- a well-known octant-degeneracy tension in current global neutrino fits. We report the 'with SK' (fully global) value as primary.

name: sin^2(theta_13)
source: NuFIT 6.0 (2024) global fit, Normal Ordering, table including Super-Kamiokande atmospheric data (nu-fit.org/sites/default/files/v60.tbl-parameters.pdf)
notes: Best fit 0.02215 (+0.00056/-0.00058); reactor mixing angle, dominated by Daya Bay/RENO/Double Chooz.

name: delta_CP
source: NuFIT 6.0 (2024) global fit, Normal Ordering, table including Super-Kamiokande atmospheric data (nu-fit.org/sites/default/files/v60.tbl-parameters.pdf): delta_CP = 212(+26/-41) degrees
notes: Converted from best-fit 212 deg (+26/-41 deg) to radians (212 deg = 3.700098... rad); rel_unc uses the average of the asymmetric 1-sigma bounds (33.5 deg = 0.5847 rad). Uncertainty is large enough that CP conservation (delta_CP = 0 or pi) is not excluded at 1 sigma. Digits=0 correctly flags that essentially no digit beyond the leading one is trustworthy.

name: m_c(m_c)/m_b(m_b)
source: PDG 2024 Summary Tables (rpp2024-sum-quarks.pdf): m_c(m_c) [MS-bar] = 1.2730(46) GeV (CL=90%), m_b(m_b) [MS-bar] = 4.183(7) GeV (CL=90%)
notes: Both masses are quoted by PDG at 90% CL rather than 1-sigma; converted to 1-sigma by dividing by 1.645 (Gaussian z-value) before combining in quadrature. Flagged as reduced-precision per task instructions since quark masses carry non-trivial renormalization-scheme and lattice-systematic uncertainties beyond simple Gaussian errors.

name: m_s/m_d
source: PDG 2024 Summary Tables (rpp2024-sum-quarks.pdf): quoted range m_s/m_d = 17-22 (CL=90%)
notes: PDG does not quote this ratio as a Gaussian central value +/- 1 sigma, only as a range at 90% CL; we take the midpoint (19.5) as central value and convert the half-width (2.5) to 1-sigma by dividing by 1.645, giving rel_unc ~7.8%. Flagged as low-precision per task instructions. Cross-check: combining PDG's separately-quoted ratios m_u/m_d = 0.462(20) (CL90%) and m_s/m_ud = 27.33(+0.18/-0.14) (CL90%) gives a derived m_s/m_d ~ 20.0 with ~1% precision, notably tighter than the directly-quoted range; we report the directly-quoted range as the more conservative, directly-sourced PDG statement rather than our own multi-equation derivation.
